title: Humanoid robot designed ‘for a lifetime’ of companionship unveiled
one_line: UBTech unveiled the U1, a mass-production humanoid robot with emotional AI designed for lifelong companionship.
why: Another entrant stakes its claim on the emotional-companion niche, a category more marketed than proven. The gap between a demo that charms and a machine someone loves for years remains vast, and this launch does little to close it.

title: Human-Level Hands? 1X Just Gave Humanoid Robot Neo Something Close
one_line: 1X upgraded its Neo humanoid robot with more dexterous hands for human-level precision and touch sensitivity.
why: Hands are the hardest part of the humanoid problem, and dexterity is where the vaporware gets separated from the useful. If Neo's new fingers can genuinely fold laundry or handle fragile objects, 1X inches the whole field closer to homes rather than stages.

title: The first commercial human-like robot is here. Are replicants next?
one_line: A startup shipped its first humanoid robot to customers, raising questions about machines becoming servants or competitors.
why: Shipping a humanoid to paying customers, even a handful, marks the shift from lab curiosity to commercial product. The philosophical hand-wringing about servants versus rivals matters less than the mundane question of what the thing can actually do once it's unboxed.

title: This humanoid robotics company is going public, but its CEO isn't promising a robot in your home anytime soon
one_line: Agility Robotics, led by Peggy Johnson, is going public via SPAC merger, becoming the first pure-play humanoid robotics company to trade publicly.
why: A humanoid company reaching public markets while its own CEO tamps down the home-robot fantasy is a rare moment of candor in a hype-drunk sector. Public investors will now get to price the distance between the promise and the shipping manifest.

title: Mitsubishi joins global robot race, plans to manufacture, deploy humanoid workers in 2027
one_line: Mitsubishi enters the humanoid robotics race with plans to manufacture and deploy robotic workers by 2027.
why: When a $200 billion industrial conglomerate commits to building and deploying humanoids, the field gains manufacturing muscle the startups lack. Mitsubishi's 2027 target puts it in direct competition with Tesla and the venture-funded pack, and factory floors are where these robots have the clearest business case.

title: Ex-Tesla Optimus scientist unveils European humanoid robot startup
one_line: Rémi Cadène, who led AI systems at Tesla's Optimus lab, is launching UMA, a Paris-based humanoid robot startup targeting Europe's aging workforce and industrial warehouses.
why: The Optimus diaspora continues, with Tesla alumni seeding rival ventures the way PayPal's did a generation ago. A European base is a bet that the continent's factories want a homegrown alternative rather than an American or Chinese one.

title: Waymo launching robotaxi service in Denver. But only for its own workers.
one_line: Waymo is quietly testing its Denver robotaxi service on employees before facing actual customers.
why: Testing on employees lets Waymo gather Denver's altitude-and-snow data without exposing paying riders to the learning curve. It's the cautious playbook that has let the company outlast flashier rivals; the tell will be how quickly the captive audience gives way to the public.

title: Waymo to start driverless rides in 4 more U.S. markets as expansion accelerates
one_line: Waymo is pushing into four new cities as the company turns its longest-running robotaxi experiment into something resembling a real business.
why: Four new markets at once signals Waymo has moved from proving the concept to scaling it, the phase where unit economics finally get scrutinized. Every city added widens the moat against Cruise's wreckage and Tesla's still-unproven robotaxi promises.

title: Waymo's vehicles ready to drive themselves in San Diego, company says
one_line: Waymo is expanding its autonomous taxi service to San Diego, another notch in its methodical march toward mainstream driverless operations.
why: San Diego joins the roster as Waymo methodically stitches together a national footprint one metro at a time. The consistency is the story: no grand announcements, just a widening map that competitors now have to catch.

title: Waymo comes to Tampa — who is responsible?
one_line: Waymo's arrival in Tampa raises immediate questions about liability when self-driving cars inevitably collide with the city's notoriously aggressive drivers.
why: Tampa's arrival surfaces the liability question that shadows every driverless deployment: when a robot and a human trade paint, whose insurer pays? These are the unglamorous legal precedents that will shape the industry as much as any software update.

title: BayCare Selects Zipline to Build Drone Delivery Network Across Tampa Bay
one_line: BayCare, Florida's largest health system, taps Zipline to deploy autonomous drones for moving blood and medical supplies across Tampa Bay.
why: Blood and medical supplies are drone delivery's genuine killer app, where minutes matter and roads don't. BayCare's endorsement gives Zipline a marquee health-system deployment and a template other hospital networks will study.

title: ZenaTech begins development of sphere-shaped autonomous drone
one_line: ZenaTech is building a spherical autonomous drone, betting rounded geometry solves problems rectangular designs don't.
why: A spherical drone is either a clever answer to durability and safety in tight spaces or a novelty in search of a use case. ZenaTech is wagering that shape is a differentiator; the market will decide whether geometry is a feature or a gimmick.

title: Skydio crosses 1,000 drone Dock deployments in just one year
one_line: Skydio's autonomous charging dock reached 1,000 deployments in one year, validating the company's unattended flight strategy.
why: The dock, not the drone, is the quiet breakthrough: it turns a piloted gadget into an unattended sentry that recharges itself. A thousand deployments in a year suggests the autonomous-inspection market is maturing from pilot projects into standing infrastructure.

title: Beyond the Helicopter: DJI’s EV50 drone brings autonomous logistics to the slopes of Mount Everest
one_line: DJI's heavy-lift EV50 drone is being tested to ferry supplies up Everest's slopes, stress-testing autonomous mountain logistics.
why: Hauling gear up Everest is equal parts marketing and genuine engineering trial, thin air and brutal cold being unforgiving referees. If DJI's heavy-lift drone survives the mountain, the more mundane payoff is autonomous logistics for mines, farms, and construction sites everywhere else.

title: Percepto Unveils AI-Powered Inspection Intelligence Platform for Energy Infrastructure and Autonomous Drone Operations
one_line: Percepto launches an AI-powered inspection platform enabling drones to autonomously monitor power grids without human pilots.
why: The value in drone inspection is migrating from the flying to the interpreting, and Percepto is planting its flag on the analysis layer. For utilities managing aging grids, software that flags a failing transformer before it burns is worth more than the aircraft that photographs it.

title: Pentagon awards $80M task order for AI-enabled tech to defend Air Force bases against small drones
one_line: AeroVironment wins an $80.5 million task order to deploy AI-powered drone-detection systems at Air Force bases.
why: The $80 million counter-drone order is the flip side of the cheap-drone revolution: every advance in unmanned attack invites an equal spend on defense. Air Force bases are learning what Ukraine's battlefields already proved, that a $500 quadcopter can threaten billion-dollar assets.

title: Ondas to Buy Military Drone Maker DZYNE for $875.8 Million
one_line: Ondas is acquiring military drone maker DZYNE for $875.8 million to expand its defense sector presence.
why: At nearly $876 million, this acquisition is the week's biggest check and a bet that defense budgets will keep favoring unmanned systems. Consolidation is coming to the military drone sector, and Ondas is positioning to be a buyer rather than a target.

title: Develop Humanoid Robot Policies End-to-End with NVIDIA Isaac GR00T | NVIDIA Technical Blog
one_line: NVIDIA's Isaac GR00T software pipeline enables training humanoid robots for complex physical tasks without simulation code.
why: By handing developers a ready-made pipeline for training humanoid behaviors, NVIDIA cements its position as the picks-and-shovels supplier to the entire robot rush. Every startup that builds on GR00T deepens dependence on NVIDIA's stack, the same playbook that made the company indispensable to generative AI.

title: US scientists use Chinese humanoid robot to carry out keyhole surgery
one_line: Unitree Robotics' Chinese humanoid robot successfully performed minimally invasive keyhole surgery in a demonstration by American surgeons.

title: The Safety Data on Autonomous Vehicles
source: Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health
one_line: Johns Hopkins researchers examine crash data to evaluate autonomous vehicle safety claims and regulatory gaps.

title: 3D-printed interceptor aircraft counters drone swarms at 350 mph
one_line: SkyDefense's 3D-printed CobraJet interceptor hunts hostile drone swarms at 350 mph, armed with compact guided rounds to replace costly air-defense missiles.

title: Experimental German Drone Hits 699 Km/h in Push Toward Future High-Speed Interceptors
one_line: Quantum Systems Group's experimental Apex Recordhunter drone hit 699 km/h in level flight, a speed record meant to inform future high-speed interceptor development.

title: Israeli Firms Pair LiDAR With AI-Driven Analytics to Sharpen Drone Detection
one_line: Innoviz Technologies and Cogniteam paired LiDAR with AI analytics to detect drones more accurately by analyzing shape, motion, and three-dimensional characteristics.

title: Etzioni on AI: Elon Musk promised humanoid robots, but China delivered
one_line: Oren Etzioni argues Chinese manufacturers are delivering humanoid robots to factories while Elon Musk promises them.

title: The humanoid robot boom is here. These top Silicon Valley investors aren't buying it.
one_line: Silicon Valley's most skeptical investors argue that humanoid robots are an inefficient solution, backing wheeled and specialized alternatives instead.

title: Free Waymo Rides in California? You Can Thank a Regulatory Quirk
one_line: Waymo operates free robotaxi rides in California by exploiting a regulatory permitting loophole.

title: 6 Best Drone Stocks for 2026 and How to Invest
one_line: AeroVironment leads a slate of drone stocks positioned to capitalize on a market expected to swell to $147.8 billion by 2036.

title: Atlas, the humanoid robot, delivers match ball at World Cup
one_line: Boston Dynamics' Atlas humanoid robot delivered the match ball at the World Cup, cementing robots as acceptable stadium entertainment.

title: Forsyth Co. Sheriff showcases humanoid robot that could help with hostage negotiations, breach doors
one_line: North Carolina's Forsyth County Sheriff demonstrated a $75,000 humanoid robot that could negotiate hostages, breach doors, and dance at crime scenes.

title: Mitsubishi Motors and Highlanders Sign MOU to Establish a New Industrial Foundation Where Humans and Robots Work Together
one_line: Mitsubishi Motors and Highlanders formalize plans to integrate robots alongside human employees in collaborative factory automation.

title: Waymo turns in teens who were drinking, shooting Orbeez from robotaxi in San Mateo
one_line: Waymo's safety systems detected minors vandalizing a robotaxi, exposing driverless fleets to human disorder beyond technical challenges.

title: Waymo cars stranded on San Francisco streets as Fourth of July gridlock drains batteries
one_line: Waymo's driverless vehicles were stranded on San Francisco streets as Fourth of July traffic drained their batteries.

title: Waymo reports teen riders for bad behavior and delivers them to the police
one_line: Waymo's autonomous vehicles have become mobile surveillance systems, reporting misbehaving passengers to law enforcement.

title: Waymo cars ran out of charge, blocked roads during SF’s July 4 fireworks
one_line: Waymo's autonomous vehicles created a traffic jam on Independence Day when their batteries died mid-route, leaving the cars stranded on San Francisco streets.

title: Waymo keeps blocking SF firefighters, and they’re fed up
one_line: Waymo's autonomous vehicles are creating dangerous delays for San Francisco fire trucks responding to emergencies.

title: First-Ever Fully Autonomous Interceptor Drone Emerges in Ukraine
one_line: Ukrainian drone maker F-Drones unveiled LITAVR, claiming it as the first fully autonomous interceptor that hunts and locks onto targets with minimal human input.

title: XTEND Secures U.S. Patent for Autonomous Drone Navigation Technology, Strengthening Next-Generation UAS Capabilities
one_line: XTEND secured a U.S. patent for autonomous drone navigation technology to strengthen next-generation unmanned aircraft capabilities.

title: REX Drone ETF Sees Inflows as Defense Spending Climbs
one_line: A drone-focused exchange-traded fund attracted investor money as Pentagon defense budgets increased.

title: Germany’s New Stealth Drone Packs 950-Km Reach Into Blended-Wing Airframe
one_line: German firm Quantum Cyber is developing PHANTOM-950, a stealthy long-range drone with blended-wing design capable of flying 950 kilometers.

title: India Gives Drone Launches a Faster Exit Strategy
one_line: Indian defense firm N3X Defence mounted its WRATH drones on mobile launchers designed for rapid deployment and retreat.

title: 'World's 1st mass-produced humanoid robot' motors to market in China
one_line: GeniusRobotics' Ares humanoid robot enters mass production in China, marking the first commercially scaled humanoid platform.
why: GeniusRobotics claims a manufacturing first that Tesla, Figure, and Unitree have all chased but not yet publicly claimed. The real test isn't the announcement but unit economics and reliability at scale — mass production means nothing if the robots can't hold up outside a showroom.

title: New York school district to deploy humanoid robot in classrooms this fall
one_line: Realbotix is deploying humanoid robots into New York school district classrooms this fall to teach STEM subjects.
why: A public school district putting humanoid robots in front of children is a real-world trust test that no lab demo can substitute for. Watch how Realbotix handles safety oversight and whether teachers actually find the robots useful, or just distracting.

title: ACE ROBOTICS Unveils Kairos 3.1 and Expands Its Embodied AI Stack from Data to Deployment at WAIC 2026
one_line: ACE Robotics unveiled Kairos 3.1, an embodied AI system spanning data collection to robot deployment, at WAIC 2026.
why: ACE Robotics is building a full-stack embodied AI system — data to deployment — that mirrors what Physical Intelligence and Figure are attempting in the US. It's another signal that China's robotics ecosystem is racing to own the entire pipeline, not just the hardware.

title: Chinese platform lets people control humanoid robots with thoughts
one_line: BrainCo unveiled a brain-computer interface platform that lets users control humanoid robots directly with their thoughts via EEG headsets.
why: BrainCo's brain-computer interface layer for humanoid control points toward teleoperation that skips joysticks and controllers entirely. It's early and gimmicky-looking now, but latency and accuracy improvements here could matter for hazardous or remote work down the line.

title: Humanoid robot lands head-high kick during ‘world’s first freestyle’ fighting in China
one_line: EngineAI's T800 humanoid robot landed a head-high kick at China's first freestyle robot fighting tournament.
why: EngineAI's fighting robot is pure spectacle, but the durability flex — a headless robot still swinging — is a marketing tactic other humanoid makers will feel pressure to match. Expect more staged combat as firms compete for viral proof of ruggedness.

title: Tesla's Robotaxi Just Launched in Miami. Here's How Small the Service Area Really Is
one_line: Tesla launched its Robotaxi service in Miami with a notably constrained operating territory.
why: Tesla's robotaxi keeps expanding, but a tiny operating footprint in Miami underscores how far the fleet remains from Waymo-scale coverage. The gap between headline launches and actual usable service area is the real story to track.

title: World-first airdrop of an autonomous drone boat into rough seas
one_line: The Royal Navy airdropped a Kraken Technologies autonomous drone boat from an RAF cargo plane into rough North Sea waters.
why: Airdropping an autonomous drone boat from a cargo plane into rough North Sea waters is a real test of rapid-deployment logistics, not a lab exercise. If it works reliably, it changes how navies think about getting unmanned assets into contested waters fast.

title: Matternet taps Beeline to accelerate US drone delivery expansion
one_line: Matternet partners with Beeline to scale drone delivery operations across the United States.
why: Matternet's partnership with Beeline is a quiet infrastructure play to scale drone delivery across the US, competing directly with Wing and Zipline for logistics contracts. Watch how fast regulatory approvals and airspace integration keep pace with expansion ambitions.

title: Anduril Autonomous Drone Fires Its First-Ever Live Missile at a Target
one_line: Anduril's autonomous drone successfully fired a live missile at a target, marking the company's first weaponized flight test.
why: Anduril's autonomous drone firing a live missile is the moment lethal autonomy stops being a slide-deck concept and becomes a demonstrated capability. It puts pressure on the Pentagon's procurement pipeline and raises the stakes for how quickly regulators and treaties can catch up.

title: A mosquito-hunting killer drone is available for preorder – and I have questions
one_line: Tornyol, a Y Combinator-backed startup, is taking preorders for an autonomous micro-drone that hunts mosquitoes by tracking their wingbeat and colliding with them mid-flight.
why: A consumer preorder for a mosquito-killing drone shows autonomous flight tech diffusing into oddball civilian niches, well beyond delivery and surveillance. The obvious question is whether it can actually target mosquitoes without misfiring on anything else that moves.

title: Autonomous Drone Fleet Manager Clears Pentagon Gate: Tesseract NOMAD Selected
one_line: Tesseract's NOMAD autonomous drone fleet management system won Pentagon selection for coordinating unmanned aircraft in contested environments.
why: Tesseract's NOMAD software winning Pentagon selection is a reminder that drone warfare's next bottleneck is coordination, not hardware. Whoever controls the fleet-management layer effectively controls how swarms behave in contested airspace.

title: US picks containerized drone system for next-gen autonomous warfare
one_line: The Pentagon tapped Kansas City-based Tesseract Ventures to develop containerized drone pods that let one operator manage multiple aircraft with minimal human intervention.
why: The Pentagon standardizing on containerized drone systems is a bet on modularity — units that can be shipped, deployed, and swapped like cargo. It's infrastructure procurement, less flashy than a missile test but arguably more consequential for how fast autonomous warfare scales.

title: Introducing Figure 03
one_line: Figure introduced Figure 03, its third-generation humanoid robot redesigned for manufacturing and trained on proprietary Helix AI.
why: Figure 03 is a redesign explicitly aimed at manufacturing scale, not lab demos, and it's trained on the company's own Helix AI rather than borrowed foundation models. This puts direct pressure on Tesla's Optimus and Unitree to show comparable production-readiness, not just impressive footage.

title: Helix 02 Living Room Tidy
one_line: Figure's Helix 02 robot tidies living rooms using coordinated tool use and learns new household tasks from video.
why: A robot that learns new household tasks straight from video, without retraining, is the generalization breakthrough the whole humanoid industry has been chasing. If it holds up outside curated demos, it's a bigger deal than any single hardware upgrade this year.

title: NEURA Robotics and Qualcomm Enter Strategic Collaboration to Advance Physical AI and Cognitive Robotics
one_line: NEURA Robotics and Qualcomm announced a partnership to develop a 'Brain + Nervous System' reference architecture for humanoid robots.
why: NEURA and Qualcomm's 'Brain + Nervous System' architecture is an attempt to standardize the chips-and-software stack that every humanoid maker currently builds in isolation. If it catches on, it becomes a real challenge to Nvidia's dominance in robotics compute.

title: Introducing Foresight
one_line: Dexterity unveiled Foresight, a world model for robotic manipulation trained on over 100 million real warehouse actions.
why: Dexterity's Foresight is a world model trained on 100 million real warehouse actions, aimed at letting robots predict physical outcomes rather than just react. It's a direct competitive shot at Physical Intelligence and Covariant in the race to make warehouse robots reason, not just grasp.

title: Wing and Papa Johns partner to build the future of fresh, hot delivery by drone
one_line: Wing and Papa Johns launched a drone-delivery pilot in Charlotte to fly hot pizza to customers via app integration.
why: Papa Johns betting on drone delivery, integrated into its own AI ordering app, signals fast food is ready to treat drones as a real logistics channel, not a novelty. Charlotte is the pilot; the real story is whether the unit economics survive scaling past one city.

title: Autonomous OGI Drones now Alternative Test Method for EPA Subpart OOOOa, OOOOb
one_line: Percepto's autonomous drone system won EPA approval as an alternative method for methane leak detection at oil and gas sites.
why: EPA approval for Percepto's autonomous methane-detection drones is a rare regulatory win that could replace manual inspection crews across oil and gas sites. It's a quiet but concrete example of autonomy actually being adopted for environmental compliance, not just demoed.

title: International Association of Campus Law Enforcement Administrators Launches National Campus Drone Readiness Program, Selecting Draganfly to Provide Drone Systems, Services and Training in Alignment with Emerging U.S. Drone Policy
one_line: Draganfly secured a contract to equip campus police departments nationwide with drones and training through a new program.

title: DRAGANFLY COMPLETES ACQUISITION OF SKIP DYNAMIX
one_line: Draganfly completed its $7.5 million acquisition of Skip Dynamix, adding low-cost fixed-wing drone capabilities.

title: Draganfly and F4 Defense International (F4DI) Selected by the Department of War for Development of Integrated, Multi-Layered, Modular, and Rapid-Deployable Counter-UAS (C-UAS) System
one_line: Draganfly and F4 Defense International won an Army contract to develop a tethered drone counter-UAS system.

title: Draganfly Launches Draganfly Blitz™ Advanced Optical Payload Product Line to Address Growing Demand for Best-in-Class, NDAA-Compliant Integrated Gimbal Systems
one_line: Draganfly launched the Blitz advanced optical payload line, NDAA-compliant EO/IR gimbal systems developed with Blitz Technologies.

title: Setting the Standard: How Aurora and McLeod are Simplifying Self-Driving Truck Adoption
one_line: Aurora and McLeod Software partnered to integrate autonomous trucks into McLeod's transportation management system, with Russell Transport adopting first.

title: Stellantis, Wayve, and Uber Partner to Scale Robotaxis Globally
one_line: Stellantis, Wayve, and Uber partnered to commercialize autonomous rides on Uber, starting with London.

title: Qualcomm and Wayve Advance Production-Ready End-to-End AI for ADAS and Automated Driving
one_line: Qualcomm and Wayve announced a production-ready end-to-end AI system for autonomous driving built on Wayve's embodied AI.

title: Joby's Commitment to Sustainable Aviation
one_line: Joby Aviation and the Reuben Brothers opened an air taxi vertiport at Century Plaza in Los Angeles, delivering 47% lower-emission eVTOL service to Orange County.

title: Xpeng Expands Beyond EVs With Munich Event, Promising Flying Cars and Humanoid Robots
one_line: Xpeng announced flying cars and humanoid robots at a Munich event, expanding beyond electric vehicles into physical AI hardware.

title: AGIBOT Unveils Four New Products at WAIC 2026, Showcasing Embodied AI in Real-World Operations
one_line: AGIBOT unveiled four new robots at Shanghai's AI conference, betting that manufacturing and factory deployment matter more than impressive demos.

title: AGIBOT X2 Series Showcases Embodied AI at the World Cup, Marking a Historic Sports Companion Debut
one_line: Agibot deployed its X2 humanoid robots at the World Cup as embodied AI systems designed to interact with crowds and assist with match operations.

title: EVERYWHERE Integrates with Skydio to Enable Autonomous Drone Response for Lone Workers
one_line: EVERYWHERE integrated Skydio's autonomous drones into its platform to dispatch aerial backup for isolated or remote workers.

title: Storm Fighter: UK christens drone wingman program, unveils new designs
one_line: The UK's Royal Air Force has christened its drone wingman program Storm Fighter, unveiling two new unmanned designs for electronic warfare and long-range attack.

title: XTEND Supports British Army During Major Autonomous Strike Exercise, Advancing UK Operational Drone Warfare Capabilities
one_line: XTEND deployed autonomous drone technology in a major British Army exercise, advancing operational integration of unmanned systems in UK military operations.

title: Austin drone boat maker Saronic picks Texas for high tech $3.2B shipyard
one_line: Saronic, an Austin drone boat maker, is building a $3.2 billion shipyard in Texas to manufacture autonomous vessels at scale.

title: F.03 Arrives at BMW
one_line: Figure deployed its F.03 humanoid robot at BMW's South Carolina plant to handle complex sequencing tasks traditional automation cannot manage.

title: Beyond the Hype
one_line: Agility Robotics' Digit humanoid robot passed OSHA-recognized safety testing at an Amazon fulfillment site, clearing deployment in human-shared warehouse environments.

title: Digit Moves Over 100,000 Totes in Commercial Deployment
one_line: Agility Robotics' Digit humanoid has moved over 100,000 totes at a GXO logistics facility, marking the first six-figure commercial throughput milestone for the general-purpose robot.

title: NEURA Robotics Showcases Full-Stack Robotics Platform at Automate 2026
one_line: NEURA Robotics will demonstrate its full-stack cognitive robotics platform and humanoid robots at Automate 2026, backed by a record $1.4 billion Series C.

title: NEURA Robotics and Dassault Systèmes Announce Partnership to Scale Physical AI Through Virtual Twins and Real World Learning
one_line: NEURA Robotics and Dassault Systèmes are linking virtual simulation with real-world robotics to train physical AI systems at industrial scale.

title: NEURA Robotics celebrates Bundesliga Premiere: Humanoid Robot debuts at the Stadium
one_line: NEURA Robotics deployed its 4NE1 Mini humanoid robot at a Bundesliga match, marking the first on-field professional sports appearance by the cognitive machine.

title: NEURA Robotics and the Technical University of Munich launch Europe’s largest scientific training center for Physical AI
one_line: NEURA Robotics and the Technical University of Munich are opening Europe's largest Physical AI training center at Munich Airport, deploying humanoid robots to generate training data.

title: Solving Dexterity: A Full-Stack Approach
one_line: Mimic Robotics unveiled the M1 tendon-driven robotic hand paired with an exoskeleton wearable for capturing human demonstrations to train dexterous manipulation.

title: Dexterity Collaborates with Beckhoff USA on Advanced Automation, Networking, and Safety for State-of-the-Art Mech Robots
one_line: Dexterity, valued at $1.65 billion and racing toward IPO, locked in Beckhoff USA as key supplier for control systems powering its Mech superhumanoid robots in logistics.

title: Dexterity and ASRock Rack Partner to Deliver Physical AI Supercomputers for Superhumanoid Edge Intelligence
one_line: Dexterity and ASRock Rack are embedding custom edge AI supercomputers into Dexterity's Mech warehouse robot to enable real-time manipulation without cloud dependency.

title: Wing extends service hours in Charlotte and Dallas-Fort Worth
one_line: Wing obtained FAA approval to deliver after dark in Charlotte and Dallas-Fort Worth, operating until 9 p.m. using infrared technology.

title: Greater Houston kicks off Walmart and Wing's biggest expansion year yet
one_line: Wing and Walmart launch drone delivery across Greater Houston, expanding their partnership to serve one of the nation's largest metros as part of what the companies call their most ambitious growth year yet.

title: Skydio Commits USD3.5 Billion to Expand U.S. Manufacturing and Secure American Drone Leadership
one_line: Skydio commits $3.5 billion to expand U.S. manufacturing and position itself as a domestic alternative to Chinese drone dominance.

title: 1000 Skydio Docks Deployed
one_line: Skydio has deployed 1,000 automated docking stations, reaching four million autonomous flights across police, utilities, and border security.

title: Vancouver Police Department Drone as First Responder
one_line: Vancouver Police Department deployed Canada's first dock-based drone-as-first-responder system using Skydio's X10 and DFR Command software.

title: US Air Force X10D EOD Follow On Contract
one_line: Skydio won a multi-million-dollar follow-on contract from the U.S. Air Force to expand its X10D drone program for explosive ordnance disposal.

title: Percepto launches next-generation inspection intelligence for energy infrastructure
one_line: Percepto launched a next-generation drone inspection platform for energy infrastructure, combining autonomous flight with AI-driven asset analysis.

title: Autonomous Methane Detection Drones Earn EPA Approval for Oil and Gas Compliance
one_line: Percepto's autonomous methane-detection drone system wins EPA approval as an Alternative Test Method, clearing the way for remote optical inspections across oil and gas sites without field crews.

title: Percepto’s New Solution Delivers Real-Time Emissions Insights at Scale
one_line: Percepto launched an AI system to analyze drone-collected emissions data in real time, with Chevron running pilot tests in U.S. oil fields.

title: Percepto launches AI emission detector for remote, drone-based methane surveying
one_line: Percepto launched an AI-powered emission detector that autonomously identifies methane leaks from drone-captured video, now deployed across major U.S. oil and gas basins.

title: Introducing Waymo Premier, an elevated rider experience
one_line: Waymo launches Premier, a $29.99-per-month membership tier offering priority pickups and cash-back rewards to frequent riders in three cities.

title: Summer break is here, but we're keeping our prover
one_line: Waymo is launching fully driverless operations in Denver, Las Vegas, San Diego, and Tampa, and testing its sixth-generation system in Hyundai IONIQ 5 vehicles.

title: Starting today, anyone in Nashville can download t
one_line: Waymo opens its Nashville driverless service to the general public, having ferried tens of thousands of pre-registered riders since April.

title: The world's largest 24/7 autonomous ride-hailing s
one_line: Waymo is expanding its autonomous ride-hailing service to 11 U.S. cities covering over 1,400 square miles, including new launches in Miami, Austin, Atlanta, and Houston ahead of the FIFA World Cup.

title: Waymo is coming to the Rose City! Portland has alw
one_line: Waymo will begin testing autonomous vehicles in Portland, Oregon, starting with manual drives to map infrastructure before pursuing regulatory approval.

title: The Road Never Sleeps: Aurora's Trucks Go Driverless Day and Night
one_line: Aurora announced driverless truck operations around the clock on U.S. highways, extending its Dallas-Houston route to overnight runs and planning a 1,000-mile Phoenix expansion by year-end.

title: Uber, Nuro, and Lucid to Bring Robotaxi Service to Houston in 2027
one_line: Uber, Nuro, and Lucid plan to launch a robotaxi service in Houston by mid-2027, their second market after San Francisco.

title: Nuro Expands to Germany, Establishing a European Base for Its Universal Autonomy Platform
one_line: Nuro opens a German headquarters to test and scale its Level 4 autonomous-driving platform in Europe, pivoting from delivery robots to autonomy tech.

title: Nuro Secures CPUC Permit for Testing Robotaxi Passenger Service in California
one_line: Nuro has secured a California Public Utilities Commission permit to test its robotaxi passenger service, clearing a regulatory hurdle for its partnership with Uber and Lucid.

title: 2025 Impact Report
one_line: Joby Aviation logged over 850 test flights in 2025 and announced a Virgin Atlantic partnership to launch commercial air taxi services in the UK.

title: Joby Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
one_line: Joby Aviation completed its first point-to-point eVTOL flights in New York City and was selected for FAA's integration pilot program across eleven states.

title: Integrating eVTOLs at Commercial Airports
one_line: Joby Aviation is conducting flight demonstrations between Manhattan and JFK as part of the FAA's eVTOL Integration Pilot Program, moving airport transfers from concept to regulatory proof-of-concept.

title: Joby Brings Electric Air Taxis to New York City in Week-Long Flight Campaign
one_line: Joby Aviation completed New York City's first point-to-point eVTOL air taxi demonstration flights, operating between JFK and Manhattan heliports as part of the FAA's integration pilot program.
companies: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey

title: Reuben Brothers Unveil Joby Vertiport at Century Plaza
one_line: Joby Aviation and Reuben Brothers opened a vertiport at a Los Angeles residential tower, marking the first in-building air taxi service for a residential development.

title: Second Gen 6 Aircraft Accelerates Wisk's Flight Test Program
one_line: Wisk Aero flew its second Generation 6 autonomous aircraft, doubling flight-test capacity to accelerate toward certified air-taxi service.

title: Wisk and Texas Selected by White House to Lead the Safe Introduction of Autonomous Air Taxi Flight
one_line: Wisk Aero wins White House backing to operate autonomous air taxis in Texas as the FAA's lead eVTOL partner.

title: EHang EH216-S Completes First Pilotless Flights in Switzerland, Supporting Advanced Air Mobility in the Alpine Region
one_line: EHang completed the first pilotless flights of its EH216-S eVTOL aircraft in Switzerland, expanding operations to 22 countries.

title: SAIC Mobility starts purpose-built Robotaxi project, targets 2027 debut
one_line: SAIC Mobility is building a purpose-designed robotaxi from scratch, targeting a 2027 debut.

title: MINIEYE, StarCharge partner on automated charging hubs for driverless fleets
one_line: MINIEYE and StarCharge have partnered to deploy automated charging hubs designed for driverless vehicle fleets.

title: Saronic autonomous drone helps rescue downed US pilots
one_line: Saronic's autonomous drone was deployed to locate and rescue downed U.S. pilots in a real-world operation.

title: Autonomous Cargo Drone Network Accelerates NHS Pathology Logistics
one_line: An autonomous cargo drone network is ferrying pathology samples across NHS hospital sites, replacing courier runs that once took hours.

title: Autonomous drone delivery companies Manna, Matternet expand US operations
one_line: Drone delivery companies Manna and Matternet are expanding their autonomous operations across the U.S.

title: Introducing Helix 02: Full-Body Autonomy
one_line: Figure unveiled Helix 02, a humanoid robot controlled by a single neural network that autonomously completes kitchen tasks without human intervention.

title: Ramping Figure 03 Production
one_line: Figure has scaled production of its Figure 03 humanoid to one robot per hour at its BotQ facility, a twenty-four-fold throughput increase.

title: Figure Signs Agreement with Catalyst Brands to Scale Humanoid Operations
one_line: Figure deploys its humanoid robots into Catalyst Brands' Nevada distribution center in its first commercial retail-logistics deployment.

title: Helix-02 Bedroom Tidy
one_line: Figure demonstrates two Helix-02 humanoids coordinating to tidy a bedroom using a single learned vision-language-action policy, with each robot inferring its partner's intent from motion alone rather than through explicit communication.

title: F.02 Contributed to the Production of 30,000 Cars at BMW
one_line: Figure AI's F.02 humanoid robot contributed to 30,000 BMW X3 vehicles at the automaker's South Carolina plant with 99%-plus accuracy before retirement.

title: NEURA Robotics and Amazon Web Services Enter Strategic Collaboration to Accelerate Physical AI at Scale
one_line: NEURA Robotics and Amazon Web Services announced a strategic partnership to accelerate physical-AI training and explore robot deployment in Amazon fulfillment centers.

title: Introducing Instinct
one_line: Dexterity deployed Instinct, a tactile AI system that guides robotic hands through box-packing tasks with an 81% production success rate.

title: EPA approves Percepto’s autonomous OGI drones for federal emissions inspections
one_line: Percepto won EPA approval to deploy autonomous drones equipped with optical gas imaging for federal emissions monitoring.

title: Joby Aviation and Toyota Motor Corporation Launch Initial Phase of a Strategic Manufacturing Alliance to Realize Air Mobility for All
one_line: Joby Aviation and Toyota Motor Corporation established a joint venture to scale production of electric air taxis combining aircraft design with manufacturing expertise.

title: Robotics startup Humanoid raises $152 million Series A round at $1.35 billion valuation
one_line: Humanoid closed a $152 million Series A round at a $1.35 billion valuation to scale physical AI robotics.

title: NEURA Robotics Announces Record Series C of up to $1.4 Billion to Accelerate the World’s Leading Physical AI Platform
one_line: NEURA Robotics closed a $1.4 billion Series C, the largest funding round for a full-stack robotics company, backed by Amazon, NVIDIA, Qualcomm, and Bosch.

title: A new AI model wants self-driving cars to think before they swerve
one_line: Seoul National University's SafeDrive model teaches self-driving cars to reason through emergency maneuvers rather than simply mimicking human reactions.

title: Dexterity Supercharges Foresight by 17x
one_line: Dexterity achieved a 17-fold speedup in its Foresight world model through NVIDIA optimization, enabling real-time dual-arm robotic manipulation at production scale.

title: Report: Waymo Cars Mean Way Mo’ Traffic
one_line: A report claims Waymo's driverless vehicles are contributing to increased congestion in New York City.

title: Tesla's jump in sales comes as Optimus, Robotaxi bets require more cash: Q2 earnings preview
one_line: Tesla's Q2 sales gains are overshadowed by capital demands from its Optimus humanoid robot and Robotaxi ambitions.

title: Tesla ‘Full Self-Driving’ got me a speeding ticket, and Tesla killed the fix
one_line: A Tesla driver received a speeding ticket blamed on Full Self-Driving after Tesla discontinued a speed-limiting feature.

title: You may never ride in an Austin robotaxi. Its mistakes could still affect you
one_line: Waymo's and Cruise's robotaxi operations in Austin create traffic and safety risks extending beyond passengers.

title: Waymo And Uber Support Bad, Incumbent-Protecting Laws In DC And NJ
one_line: Waymo and Uber are backing regulatory measures in Washington D.C. and New Jersey that critics say protect incumbents over innovation.

title: Bill Ackman Says Tesla Robotaxi Fears Are Creating a 'Very Cheap' Buying Opportunity for UBER
one_line: Bill Ackman argues Tesla's robotaxi fears have undervalued Uber, creating a compelling investment opportunity.

title: Opinion | A.I. Drones Are Coming. We Are Not Ready.
one_line: Regulatory and safety frameworks lag behind advances in autonomous drone technology.

title: At SIGGRAPH, NVIDIA Advances Graphics and Simulation With Agentic and Physical AI
one_line: NVIDIA showcases graphics and simulation tools for physical AI applications at SIGGRAPH.

title: Could humanoid robots be used to fire mortars? | Defense Dollars
one_line: An unnamed robotics startup explores whether humanoid robots could eventually perform dangerous military tasks like breaching doors and firing mortars.

title: Are Waymo robotaxis actually safer than NYC Uber and taxi drivers?
one_line: Waymo's robotaxis are examined against human drivers in New York City, with safety measurement methods determining outcomes.

title: Tesla Earnings: Wall Street Wants Proof AI, Robotaxi, Optimus Spending 'Strengthening' Company
one_line: Wall Street investors demand concrete evidence that Tesla's massive spending on driverless, robotaxi, and humanoid robotics is delivering returns rather than draining capital.

title: Waymo really wants to operate in D.C. But does D.C. want Waymo?
one_line: Waymo pushes to launch driverless operations in Washington D.C. but faces local regulatory and political resistance.

title: Humanoid Robot Market to Reach USD 50.27 Billion by 2035, Growing at 28.1% CAGR, Says MarketsandMarkets™
one_line: The humanoid robot market will balloon from $5.41 billion in 2026 to $50.27 billion by 2035, driven by labor shortages and aging populations, says MarketsandMarkets.

title: BMW Group Plant Landshut develops software for humanoid robotics in component production.
one_line: BMW's Landshut plant is developing software to teach humanoid robots to handle complex assembly tasks, partnering with startup Athenyx Robotics.

title: Tesla self-driving mode wasn't to blame in Houston-area crash, report suggests
one_line: Investigators cleared Tesla's self-driving system in a Houston-area crash, deflecting scrutiny of the company's autonomous-driving claims.

title: Beam Global Receives Drone and Autonomous Robotics Battery Orders Exceeding $0.5 Million In a Single Week
one_line: Beam Global reported over $500,000 in new orders for drone and robotics batteries within a single week.

title: NIAR partnership aims to make Kansas national leader in autonomous aviation testing
one_line: NIAR and partners are positioning Kansas as a hub for autonomous aircraft testing and development.

title: Blue Water Autonomy, Saildrone Sue US Navy Over MUSV Program
one_line: Blue Water Autonomy and Saildrone sued the U.S. Navy over a contract award for its Medium-class Uncrewed Surface Vehicle program.

title: Inside China’s big AI show: Robot police, dancing humanoids and a roof collapse
one_line: Hundreds of thousands descended on Shanghai's World Artificial Intelligence Conference to gawk at humanoid robots and dancing machines as China flexes its AI muscles.

title: Amid Fears of Killer Robots, Humanoid MMA Fight in China Ends With Decapitation
one_line: EngineAI's humanoid combat tournament in Shenzhen ended with one robot decapitating another, undercutting UN warnings about killer machines.

title: Hyundai Workers Walk Out in Auto Industry’s First Humanoid Robot Strike
one_line: Hyundai workers staged the auto industry's first strike over humanoid robots, protesting the company's deployment of machine labor on factory floors.

title: Exclusive: Waymo expects D.C. robotaxi talks to continue this summer
one_line: Waymo says it will keep talking to Washington regulators about robotaxi deployment this summer, with no deal or timeline confirmed.

title: Firefighters Had to Move a Waymo Themselves After It Blocked an Emergency Call
one_line: Waymo's driverless vehicle blocked a San Francisco fire station entrance, forcing firefighters to manually move the car during an emergency.

title: Tesla remote operator crashed a ‘Robotaxi’ in Houston, NHTSA data shows
one_line: A Tesla remote operator crashed one of the company's Robotaxi vehicles in Houston, according to NHTSA safety data.

title: DC Robotaxi Bill Omits Wheelchair Access While Waymo and Uber Clash Over Rules
one_line: Washington's proposed robotaxi regulations fail to mandate wheelchair accessibility, leaving Waymo and Uber to dispute standards in a market that may exclude disabled passengers.
